free_text: Study of statistical emissions in 28Si+natSi and
28Si+27Al reactions at 4-5 MeV/A incident energies
Physics Department, Kalyani University,
Kalyani, West Bengal
Physics Department, Utkal University,
Bhubaneswar, Orissa
All data given in fig.2 to
fig.4
Part of data given in fig.1
Spectroscopically pure, 1 mg/cm2, self-supporting
natSi and 0.8 mg/cm2 27Al were used as targets.
14 UD Pelletron
Two counter telescopes consisting
of two element, dE-E, Si solid state detectors, were
used for particle identification and energy
measurements. The thickness of dE and E were 50
microns and 3150 microns for T1 and 35 microns and
3050 microns for T2. T1 covered 54 degree, 66 degree,
102 degree and T2 covered 114 degree, 126 degree, 138
degree for natSi and 27Al targets. Monitor detectors
were setup at +/- 9.6 degree for normalization.
No information on the sources of
uncertainty is given in the article.
The theoretical analysis of the data has been
done using the statistical model code CASCADE.
Request sent to Chinmay Basu by e-mail on
2012-12-01 and no reply is received.
Compiled by R.Ghosh,B.Jyrwa,NEHU,INDIA.
